  (e.g., ceramic-handler per CF-0006, ward-wright per CF-0007, extraction-

  lantern-hold / door-keep / one-fetch roles — the pay-split uses a named
  bifurcation: the chartered professional takes the "serious share" and
  the auxiliary takes the "corner-share", a customary copper-to-lower-
  silver tier calibrated to the role-scope (lantern-hold, door-keep, one-
  fetch). The serious-share / corner-share register is canal-heartland
  oral-register for informal chartered-plus-auxiliary cellar-work pay-
  splits, distinct from sole-contractor flat-silver (CF-0024) and from
  fully-rostered chartered-extraction-team pay.

# Corner-Share: Named Informal Compensation Tier for Contractor-Auxiliary on Chartered-Plus-Auxiliary Cellar-Jobs

## What the Artifact Implies

The travelogue's seventh paragraph documents the cellar-job's pay-split with professional precision: *"I was paid in silver. Aldous got the serious share; I took the corner-share a contracted auxiliary gets when she holds the lantern and keeps the door and runs once, down a stair, for a thing the ceramic-handler had left in his bag."* Two features mark the passage as a named-register claim rather than ad-hoc metaphor: (a) the definite articles ("the serious share", "the corner-share"), deployed to a Brinewick literate reader as though reader-familiar; and (b) the explicit role-scope enumeration (lantern / door / one fetch) that the phrase "a contracted auxiliary gets" generalizes over. The narrator is describing, not inventing, a compensation register she expects her reader to recognize.

## Why It Fits This World

CF-0024 commits the contractor-auxiliary stratum with considerable institutional texture (hazard-pay asymmetry, tavern-keeper vouch, guild-commission roster, portable-reputation building). CF-0009 commits the copper-silver denomination with explicit "meaningful spreads between petty tasks (copper) and higher-risk work (silver and above)." CF-0006 commits the chartered ceramic-containment technology for which the auxiliary provides lantern-hold / door-keep / fetch support. The three institutional axes — auxiliary labor / denomination spread / chartered-plus-auxiliary configuration — intersect at a compensation-tier that CF-0024 leaves unnamed; the corner-share register fills that gap without altering any chartered-guild monopoly or invariant.

Tonal contract preserved: the naming register is dry and professional ("took the corner-share"), consistent with AES-1 "heroism is paid in coin and scars, not glory." Vespera's self-description as auxiliary (not chartered professional, not heroic adventurer) honors WORLD_KERNEL "Adventurers exist but are treated as dangerous laborers, not chosen ones."

## Narrator Reliability Assessment

Vespera is a working CF-0024 contractor-auxiliary whose dossier places her in the middle tier (above new-hire, below named-veteran). The corner-share claim is firsthand (she was paid this way in the specific cellar-job the travelogue narrates) and professionally-sourced (the vocabulary she would have acquired on the circuit). The narrator's inflation-bias primarily inflates her adventurousness and public reputation; the corner-share naming is a self-deprecating technical descriptor — she names herself auxiliary to Aldous's chartered work — which reduces inflation-risk for this specific claim relative to other travelogue content.

The artifact is sole source for the specific NAMING of the tier. CF-0024 commits contractor-auxiliary compensation practice partially; CF-0009 commits the denomination mechanics. No other artifact on disk independently names the serious-share / corner-share bifurcation. Per Phase 3 narrator-reliability mapping, firsthand + sole-source-for-the-naming caps at `soft_canon`, with the partial-support pathway sustaining it against demotion.

## Immediate Consequences

Canal-heartland contractor-auxiliary jobs using CF-0024 tavern-board posting or herb-wife-mark circuit transmission acquire recognizable serious-share / corner-share compensation vocabulary, explicit in contract-posting or implicit in tavern-vouch. Tavern-keepers accrue informal records of "corner-share-reliable" auxiliaries for repeat chartered-professional engagement — extending the CF-0024 tavern-keeper-vouch social-asset layer with a specific competence register. Circuit-bard material (per CF-0017 / CF-0024 contracted-bard narrative-custody) incorporates corner-share idiom into contractor-ballads and tavern-job-told-right register.

## Longer-Term Consequences

Contractor-auxiliary economic stratification becomes measurable: the serious-share / corner-share bifurcation names an existing tier within CF-0024 contractor labor, making the chartered-adjacent auxiliary's income-ceiling explicit and separable from sole-contractor flat-silver or fully-rostered chartered-extraction-team pay. Language feedback: "corner-share" as idiom drifts into broader tavern-register vocabulary for any junior-role-in-chartered-operation pay — a herb-wife's runner-apprentice paid "corner-share" on the household cellar-visit, say — extending beyond pure contractor-auxiliary scope into a broader chartered-adjacent informal-labor lexicon.

Charter-audit discipline in Brinewick and similarly-strict polities (per CF-0038 / CF-0043) creates a formal-vs-informal vocabulary divide: the corner-share register is tolerated in informal practice but does not appear in civic-registry contract-templates; a contractor-auxiliary working across polity lines experiences the vocabulary diverging from formalized civic-intake pay-register, a small texture-increment in the CF-0038 Brinewick charter-audit-strictness stabilizer. Widow-of-the-job absorption (per CH-0008 / CH-0010 Rule 3 precedents) lands sharper for this tier specifically: a corner-share-rank auxiliary's widow operates on a lower income-recovery base than a serious-share-rank chartered-professional's widow, extending the widow-pension-absence register-problem along a named tier.

## Risks

- **Rule 3 specialness-inflation**: the named-tier must not become a full new compensation institution; it is a named register within CF-0024 auxiliary labor, not a new stratum. Downstream prose must honor absorption discipline paralleling CH-0008 widow-of-the-raid and CH-0009 widow-of-the-hunt Rule 3 absorptions into widow-of-the-job.
- **CF-0024 scope-preservation**: the corner-share register does NOT alter the chartered-guild monopoly on extraction / ward-inscription / ceramic-vessel containment / neutralization (SOC-4 preserved). The chartered professional retains all chartered-work authority; the serious-share / corner-share bifurcation is purely a pay-split register.
- **Narrator inflation-bias**: sole-source-for-naming could reflect Vespera's self-deprecating voice-register rather than widespread circuit-vocabulary currency. Adjudicator may judge this personal-idiom-adjacent and prefer a `contested_canon` framing; Phase 3 mapping produced `soft_canon` via partial-support pathway, but the adjudicator can further calibrate.

## Likely Burden If Accepted

Low-to-moderate. `likely_required_downstream_updates` lists three files — INSTITUTIONS.md (CF-0024 annotation naming the bifurcation), ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md (pay-register texture), EVERYDAY_LIFE.md (informal-labor vocabulary in canal-heartland register). No new CF subtree; the card names a register within an existing institutional frame. `integration_burden: 1` is accurate.

## Likely Story Yield

Modest. WORLD_KERNEL §Natural Story Engines includes "Artifact-extraction job that goes wrong" and the CF-0034-extended "Ransom-negotiator / Hunter-contractor return" family — the corner-share register provides compensation-vocabulary texture for these engines without generating new ones. Specific story occasions: (i) a corner-share-rank auxiliary who judges the chartered-professional's work-order beyond her pay-tier's risk, and whether she refuses the fetch-role mid-job; (ii) a tavern-keeper withdrawing vouch from a "corner-share-unreliable" auxiliary whose failure at a chartered-plus-auxiliary cellar-job cost the chartered professional time and reputation; (iii) Brinewick formal-contract-template audit exposing a chartered-professional's informal corner-share retainer as an unregistered-compensation magistrate-docket matter (CF-0038 + CF-0043 charter-audit-strictness friction).

## Would This Be Better As

`soft_canon`. The partial-support evidence-breadth pathway sustains soft_canon; if the adjudicator judges this personal-idiom-adjacent rather than pan-circuit institutional-register, `contested_canon` is the natural demotion target. Promotion to hard_canon would require corroboration from at least one additional canal-heartland contractor-testimony source naming the bifurcation.

## Canon Safety Check Trace

**Phase 6a (Invariants)**: All 16 tested; PASS. Notable checks — SOC-4 chartered-guild monopoly preserved (corner-share is auxiliary pay-register, not chartered-work authority transfer); SOC-3 coin-contract sacred (named pay-tier honored as contract form); CAU-1 artifact-costs preserved (no cost-free-magic implication); AES-1 heroism-in-coin-and-scars preserved (dry labor-register naming).

**Phase 6b (Mystery Reserve Content Firewall)**: All 20 MR entries checked; `overlap: false` per entry (see frontmatter). M-7 checked with specific rationale — corner-share is SUB-specialist auxiliary pay, categorically distinct from M-7's occult-fallout-specialist reputation-genealogy. No shaping.

**Phase 6c (Distribution Discipline)**: Three stabilizers in `why_not_universal` — configuration-gate (chartered-plus-auxiliary only), regional-sibling-register-gate (highland / drylands / fenland sibling conventions), vocabulary-travel-limit (canal-heartland oral-register; charter-audit polities maintain formal-vocabulary separation). CFs consulted: CF-0006, CF-0009, CF-0017, CF-0024, CF-0038, CF-0043.

**Phase 6d (Diegetic-to-World Laundering)**:
- **6d.1 Evidence-breadth**: `partial` — CF-0009 + CF-0024 commit underlying mechanics; artifact specifies the naming. Proposed_status soft_canon defensible via partial-support.
- **6d.2 Epistemic-horizon**: `pass` — Vespera is working CF-0024 contractor-auxiliary; vocabulary is within professional horizon.
- **6d.3 MR Positional**: `pass` — bardic-custody + contractor-auxiliary M-7 adjacency noted; claim is about auxiliary pay-tier, not specialist reputation-genealogy. No positional flag.

**Phase 6f Repairs Applied**: None fired.
